 {"id":"bd-2elj","title":"Proptest: SSH command escaping with special chars","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:03:29.455539805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:59:40.043160837Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:59:40.043099423Z","close_reason":"Added 33 proptest tests for SSH command escaping: shell_escape_value, is_valid_env_key, build_env_prefix with security tests for injection prevention","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2elj","depends_on_id":"bd-2ziz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2f5o","title":"WS3: Replace RwLock with Atomics for Small Types in WorkerState","description":"PROBLEM: rchd/src/workers.rs WorkerState (lines 17-40) wraps 10 fields in RwLock:
  Line 19: config: RwLock<WorkerConfig>
  Line 21: status: RwLock<WorkerStatus>
  Line 25: speed_score: RwLock<f64>
  Line 27: last_latency_ms: RwLock<Option<u64>>
  Line 29: cached_projects: RwLock<Vec<String>>
  Line 31: circuit: RwLock<CircuitStats>
  Line 33: last_error_msg: RwLock<Option<String>>
  Line 35: capabilities: RwLock<WorkerCapabilities>
  Line 37: disabled_reason: RwLock<Option<String>>
  Line 39: disabled_at: RwLock<Option<i64>>

Only used_slots (line 23) already uses AtomicU32.

RwLock has ~25ns overhead per acquisition even uncontended. Worker selection reads speed_score, last_latency_ms, consecutive_failures, and total_builds for every worker during scoring.

WHY IT MATTERS: With N workers, selection does N * K lock acquisitions. AtomicU64 load with Relaxed ordering is ~1ns (single MOV on x86_64).

SOLUTION: Convert ONLY the hot-path scalar fields to atomics:
  - speed_score: RwLock<f64> → AtomicU64 (f64::to_bits/from_bits)
  - last_latency_ms: RwLock<Option<u64>> → AtomicU64 (0 = None)
  - disabled_at: RwLock<Option<i64>> → AtomicI64 (0 = None)

KEEP AS RWLOCK (complex types, not hot-path, or infrequently accessed):
  - config: RwLock<WorkerConfig> (complex struct, rarely changes)
  - status: RwLock<WorkerStatus> (enum with semantic transitions, needs write guard for TOCTOU fix)
  - cached_projects: RwLock<Vec<String>> (heap-allocated collection)
  - circuit: RwLock<CircuitStats> (multi-field struct)
  - last_error_msg: RwLock<Option<String>> (heap-allocated, diagnostic only)
  - capabilities: RwLock<WorkerCapabilities> (complex struct, set once)
  - disabled_reason: RwLock<Option<String>> (heap-allocated, diagnostic only)

NOTE: The original bead listed consecutive_failures and total_builds as RwLock fields, but they don't appear in the actual struct. Verify during implementation whether they exist elsewhere or were already atomic.

FILES: rchd/src/workers.rs (WorkerState struct + accessors), rchd/src/selection.rs (reads during scoring)
RISK: Medium — must ensure memory ordering correctness for f64 bit-casting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:31.559122805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:24.212899130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:24.212829671Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: WS3.1 (speed_score AtomicU64) CLOSED, WS3.2 (scalar RwLock replacements) CLOSED, WS3.3 (memory ordering verification) CLOSED, WS3.4 (unit tests) CLOSED. Three fields converted from RwLock to atomic: speed_score (AtomicU64 with f64 bit-casting), last_latency_ms (AtomicU64 with 0 sentinel), disabled_at (AtomicI64 with 0 sentinel).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f5o","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-2f5o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BACKGROUND: Worker selection iterates all N workers and reads speed_score, last_latency_ms, consecutive_failures, and total_builds for scoring. With RwLock, that is N * 4 lock acquisitions minimum per selection. Even uncontended, RwLock costs ~25ns per acquire+release (futex syscall on Linux). With 10 workers, that is 10 * 4 * 25ns = 1μs just in lock overhead.

AtomicU64 load with Relaxed ordering compiles to a single MOV instruction on x86_64 — effectively free (~1ns). This reduces lock overhead from ~1μs to ~40ns for the same 10-worker scenario.

CAVEAT: f64 is not natively atomic in Rust's std. The standard pattern is AtomicU64 + f64::to_bits/from_bits, which is a zero-cost transmute (same bits, different interpretation). This is well-established in the Rust ecosystem.

KEEP AS RWLOCK: last_health_check (Instant is 16 bytes, not atomic-friendly, and only accessed during periodic health checks — not on the hot path).","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:19Z"},{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2f5o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION FROM REVIEW: The original bead listed only 5 RwLock fields. The actual WorkerState struct has 10 RwLock fields plus 1 AtomicU32 (used_slots). After analysis, only 3 fields are candidates for atomic conversion (speed_score, last_latency_ms, disabled_at) — the rest are complex types (Vec, struct, Option<String>) that genuinely need RwLock.

Also: consecutive_failures and total_builds were listed in the original audit but do NOT exist as standalone fields in WorkerState. They may be tracked inside CircuitStats. This must be verified during implementation — do NOT create phantom fields.

The net impact is smaller than originally estimated (3 fields, not 5), but still worthwhile: those 3 fields are the ones read on every selection decision.","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:54:30Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2f8g","title":"Replace all bare bail\!() calls with structured errors","description":"# Replace bail\!() with Structured Errors

## Problem
There are ~35 `bail\!()` calls in commands.rs that produce user-unfriendly errors:

### Examples of Bad Errors
```rust
// Line 2167 - No context, no code, no remediation
bail\!("rch-wkr not found on remote");

// Line 2197 - Missing which worker
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());

// Line 2943 - No error code for scripting
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());

// Line 7317 - Generic message
bail\!("Daemon socket not found at {:?}", socket_path);
```

### What Users See
```
Error: rch-wkr not found on remote
```
No code to look up, no suggestion, no context.

## Solution
Replace with proper error types from rch/src/error.rs:

```rust
// Before
bail\!("rch-wkr not found on remote");

// After  
return Err(WorkerError::BinaryNotFound {
    worker_id: worker.id.to_string(),
    host: worker.host.clone(),
    binary: "rch-wkr".to_string(),
}.into());
```

## New Error Variants Needed

### WorkerError (add to error.rs)
```rust
/// Required binary not found on worker.
#[error("Binary '{binary}' not found on worker '{worker_id}' ({host})")]
#[diagnostic(
    code("RCH-E109"),
    help("Deploy the binary with: rch workers deploy-binary {worker_id}")
)]
BinaryNotFound {
    worker_id: String,
    host: String,
    binary: String,
}

/// Toolchain installation failed on worker.
#[error("Toolchain installation failed on '{worker_id}'")]
#[diagnostic(
    code("RCH-E110"),
    help("Check rustup on worker: ssh {user}@{host} rustup show")
)]
ToolchainInstallFailed {
    worker_id: String,
    host: String,
    user: String,
    stderr: String,
}
```

### DaemonError (add to error.rs)
```rust
/// Daemon socket not found.
#[error("Daemon socket not found: {path}")]
#[diagnostic(
    code("RCH-E301"),
    help("Start the daemon with: rch daemon start")
)]
SocketNotFound { path: PathBuf }
```

## Locations to Fix
| Line | Current bail\!() | New Error |
|------|-----------------|-----------|
| 2026 | Generic SSH error | SshError::CommandFailed |
| 2043 | Version check failed | WorkerError::VersionCheckFailed |
| 2167 | rch-wkr not found | WorkerError::BinaryNotFound |
| 2197 | Binary copy failed | WorkerError::DeployFailed |
| 2217 | Ownership change failed | WorkerError::PermissionError |
| 2235 | Chmod failed | WorkerError::PermissionError |
| 2256 | Symlink failed | WorkerError::SymlinkFailed |
| 2943 | rustup install failed | WorkerError::ToolchainInstallFailed |
| 2978 | Missing hostname | ConfigError::MissingEnvVar |
| 5520-5561 | Config validation | ConfigError::InvalidValue |
| 7317 | Socket not found | DaemonError::SocketNotFound |

## Testing
- Each error should have a unit test
- Error codes should be unique
- Error messages should include context (which worker, etc.)
- Help text should be actionable

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/error.rs (add new error variants)
- rch/src/commands.rs (replace bail\!() calls)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:08:05.700801475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:07.808892211Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","depends_on_id":"bd-2u2g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","depends_on_id":"bd-3b94","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rch/src/error_test.rs)
For EACH new error variant:
1. `test_error_code_format()` - Verify RCH-Exxx format
2. `test_error_has_help_text()` - Verify help() returns non-empty
3. `test_error_includes_context()` - Verify worker_id, host, etc. present
4. `test_error_display_is_user_friendly()` - No internal jargon
5. `test_error_miette_rendering()` - Verify colors, formatting

### Integration Tests
1. Trigger each error condition and verify correct error type
2. Verify JSON output includes error code
3. Verify exit codes match error categories

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_error_codes.sh)
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_error_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

# Test 1: Verify all errors have codes
log "Test 1: Checking all errors have RCH-E codes"
rch workers probe nonexistent 2>&1 | grep -q "RCH-E" || {
  log "FAIL: Error missing RCH-E code"
  exit 1
}
log "PASS: Errors have RCH-E codes"

# Test 2: Verify JSON includes error code
log "Test 2: JSON output includes error code"
ERROR_JSON=$(rch workers probe nonexistent --json 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$ERROR_JSON" | jq -e '.error.code' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  log "PASS: JSON includes error code"
else
  log "FAIL: JSON missing error code"
  exit 1
fi

# Test 3: Verify help text present
log "Test 3: Error includes remediation"
rch workers probe nonexistent 2>&1 | grep -qiE "(help|try|verify|check)" || {
  log "FAIL: Error missing remediation hints"
  exit 1
}
log "PASS: Error includes remediation"

log "All error code tests passed"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log error construction at DEBUG level with all context
- Log error code at ERROR level
- Log remediation attempts at INFO level","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:36Z"},{"id":83,"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: Reduced bail!() calls from ~35 to 23. Fixed SSH error at line 3297, CLI argument validation (lines 8959-8967), and 3 rsync failures in transfer.rs. Remaining: commands/mod.rs (5), status_display.rs (9), agent/hook.rs (4), main.rs (2), config.rs (1), completions.rs (1), hook_windows.rs (1). All tests passing (63/63).","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:44:11Z"},{"id":84,"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Final session update: Reduced bail!() calls to 18 total. Remaining are edge cases (platform stubs, unsupported features). Changed: transfer.rs (3→TransferError::SyncFailed), status_display.rs (4→DaemonError::ProtocolError), config.rs (1→ConfigError::InvalidValue). All 63 tests passing.","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:07Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2g66","title":"WA: Cross-platform CI matrix validation","description":"Add .github/workflows/ci.yml matrix that builds and tests on: linux-x64, linux-arm64, macos-x64, macos-arm64, windows-x64. Include cargo test, cargo clippy, cargo fmt checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T07:42:31.803415083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:43:28.869345791Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:43:28.869279247Z","close_reason":"Expanded CI test matrix to linux x64/arm64, macOS x64/arm64, and Windows x64 (uses GH runner labels ubuntu-24.04-arm + macos-15-intel); unified cargo test step and kept fmt/clippy/check gates.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2ga8","title":"True E2E Output Validation & Correctness Tests","description":"# Feature: True E2E Output Validation & Correctness Tests

## Purpose

Test that output from remote compilation (stdout, stderr, exit codes, terminal colors) is correctly preserved and propagated to the calling agent.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

Output validation tests MUST use structured JSON logging:

### Output-Specific Log Events

```json
// Output capture start
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_101","phase":"execute","msg":"Command executed","data":{"cmd":"cargo test","mode":"remote","worker":"css"}}

// Exit code verification
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_101","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code check","data":{"expected":101,"actual":101,"match":true,"semantic":"tests_failed"}}

// stdout/stderr comparison
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_stderr_preservation","phase":"verify","msg":"Output comparison","data":{"stream":"stderr","local_bytes":1234,"remote_bytes":1234,"match":true,"hash_local":"abc123","hash_remote":"abc123"}}

// Color code detection
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_ansi_colors","phase":"verify","msg":"ANSI codes detected","data":{"total_codes":15,"colors":["red","green","yellow"],"styles":["bold","underline"]}}

// Streaming latency
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_streaming","phase":"timing","msg":"First byte latency","data":{"local_first_byte_ms":50,"remote_first_byte_ms":85,"overhead_ms":35}}

// Mismatch details (on failure)
{"ts":"...","level":"ERROR","test":"test_output_mismatch","phase":"verify","msg":"Output mismatch detected","data":{"stream":"stdout","line":42,"local":"expected text","remote":"actual text","diff":"<unified diff snippet>"}}
```

### Metrics to Log

1. **Exit codes**: expected, actual, semantic meaning
2. **Output size**: bytes per stream, truncation if any
3. **Content hash**: SHA-256 of local vs remote
4. **ANSI codes**: detected codes, colors, styles
5. **Timing**: first byte latency, total duration
6. **Diff details**: on mismatch, show context

## Why This Matters

- AI agents parse compilation output to understand errors and fix code
- Corrupted or missing output makes agents unable to diagnose issues
- Terminal colors help agents distinguish warnings from errors
- Exit codes determine whether agents retry or report failure

## Output Types to Verify

### Exit Code Semantics

1. **Exit 0** = success (all tests passed, build succeeded)
   - Log: semantic="success", verify stdout contains success indicators
   
2. **Exit 1** = compilation/build error
   - Log: semantic="build_error", verify error message in stderr
   
3. **Exit 101** = cargo test: tests ran but some failed
   - Log: semantic="tests_failed", count failed tests in output
   
4. **Exit 128+N** = killed by signal N
   - Log: signal number, signal name
   
5. **All non-zero** denies local re-execution
   - Log: verify no local fallback on compilation error

### stdout/stderr Preservation

1. **Compiler error messages** (line numbers, context)
   - Log: line number parsed correctly, context preserved
   
2. **Test output** (pass/fail indicators, assertion messages)
   - Log: test count, pass/fail breakdown
   
3. **Warning messages** (not lost or mixed)
   - Log: warning count, no interleaving corruption
   
4. **Progress indicators** (cargo build percentage)
   - Log: progress updates received
   
5. **Multi-line output** (stack traces, long errors)
   - Log: line count, no truncation

### Terminal Formatting

1. **ANSI color codes preserved** (TERM, NO_COLOR, CARGO_TERM_COLOR)
   - Log: TERM value, color codes detected, preservation status
   
2. **Bold/underline for emphasis**
   - Log: style codes found
   
3. **Color-coded test results** (green pass, red fail)
   - Log: pass=green, fail=red verification
   
4. **cargo's progress bar and spinner**
   - Log: carriage returns, spinner frames
   
5. **Wide characters and unicode**
   - Log: unicode points found, correct rendering

### Streaming Behavior

1. **Output streams in real-time** (not batched at end)
   - Log: timestamp of first byte, timestamp of last byte
   
2. **stderr and stdout interleaved correctly**
   - Log: interleaving order preserved
   
3. **Large output (>1MB) handled** without truncation
   - Log: total bytes, no truncation marker
   
4. **Binary data in output** doesn't corrupt stream
   - Log: binary bytes detected, stream integrity

## Merged E2E Scenarios (Hook/Feature-Flag/Error Display)

### Hook Protocol Integrity (from bd-3obh)

1. **Hook JSON response remains pristine**
   - Run: `echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}' | rch hook --stdin`
   - Verify stdout is valid JSON (no ANSI), stderr only for rich output
   - Log: json_valid=true, ansi_present=false, stderr_bytes

2. **Compilation output passthrough**
   - Run: `rch compile cargo build` on a fixture with known output
   - Verify stdout/stderr identical to local execution (byte-for-byte after normalization)
   - Log: stdout/stderr hashes, diff on mismatch

### Rich Output Gating & Feature Flags (from bd-dej3)

1. **Compile-time rich-ui off**
   - Build without rich-ui feature flag
   - Verify no rich formatting emitted anywhere
   - Log: build_flags, ansi_present=false

2. **Runtime gating**
   - With rich-ui on, ensure `--json` output is still plain JSON and stderr-only rich output
   - With `NO_COLOR=1`, ensure ANSI stripped
   - Log: TERM/NO_COLOR values and ansi detection

### Error Display Verification (from bd-vp67)

1. **SSH connection refused display**
   - Expected: error code, host/port, remediation hint
   - Verify NO_COLOR removes ANSI

2. **Config parse error display**
   - Show file path, line number, and snippet context

3. **Build failure display**
   - Compiler error text preserved verbatim

4. **JSON error mode**
   - `--json` outputs structured error to stdout
   - Error object includes code + message + remediation (if available)

5. **Severity differentiation**
   - Warning vs error output visually distinct (icons/text), no crash

6. **stderr-only guarantee**
   - Non-JSON errors never pollute stdout (stdout empty or valid JSON)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Exit codes match local execution
- [ ] stdout/stderr byte-for-byte identical (after normalization)
- [ ] Colors preserved when TERM is set
- [ ] Streaming works for long builds
- [ ] No output corruption for any test case
- [ ] Hook JSON responses are valid and ANSI-free
- [ ] Rich output never contaminates stdout in --json mode
- [ ] Error displays include code + remediation and respect NO_COLOR
- [ ] ALL tests emit structured JSON logs
- [ ] Mismatches logged with detailed diffs

## Technical Notes

- Use std::process::Output for capture
- Test with various TERM values (xterm-256color, dumb, etc)
- Verify against local execution baseline
- Log raw byte sequences for debugging

## Dependencies

- Requires bd-3saj (test infrastructure)
- Requires bd-12hi (cargo compilation produces output to verify)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:13.741339923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T07:21:59.688655882Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T07:19:35.572737775Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","depends_on_id":"bd-12hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","depends_on_id":"bd-1cwg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"StormyAnchor: Created E2E output validation tests (scripts/e2e_bd-2ga8.sh). 13 tests covering exit codes, stdout/stderr, hook JSON, ANSI colors, large output, error display. All use structured JSONL logging.","created_at":"2026-01-26T07:21:59Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3dv2","title":"Epic: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH","description":"# Epic: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH

## Executive Summary

This epic encompasses the complete integration of the rich_rust library into RCH (Remote Compilation Helper) to provide beautiful, premium terminal output for human operators watching AI coding agents work. The integration must maintain **zero interference** with AI agents, which are the primary users of RCH.

## Background & Motivation

### The Problem
RCH currently outputs plain text for status, errors, and progress. While functional, this provides a poor experience for humans monitoring AI agents. When operators run 15+ agents simultaneously, they need at-a-glance visual feedback about:
- Which workers are healthy/degraded/offline
- What compilations are in progress
- Where errors occurred and how to fix them
- Transfer and benchmark progress

### The Solution
Integrate rich_rust (a Rust port of Python's Rich library) to provide:
- Styled text with markup syntax ([bold red]text[/])
- Tables with auto-sizing columns and Unicode borders
- Panels for framed content (errors, status)
- Progress bars for transfers and benchmarks
- Color-coded status indicators
- Automatic terminal capability detection and color downgrade

## The Cardinal Rule: Agent-First Architecture

**CRITICAL**: AI coding agents are the PRIMARY users of RCH. They interact through:
1. JSON hook protocol (stdin -> stdout)
2. Exit codes (0, 1, 101, 128+N)
3. Streamed compilation output

Rich formatting must NEVER interfere with these machine interfaces. The design principle is:
- **stderr for humans**: Rich output goes to stderr
- **stdout for machines**: Structured data stays pristine on stdout
- **Graceful degradation**: If rich_rust fails, fall back to plain text

## Output Classification

### NEVER Add Rich Formatting (Machine Output)
- Hook JSON responses (stdout)
- --json flag output (stdout)
- Compilation output passthrough (stdout)
- Test output streaming (stdout)
- Exit codes (process exit status)

### ADD Rich Formatting (Human Output)
- rch status commands (stderr)
- rch workers list/probe/benchmark (stderr)
- rchd startup banner and health (stderr)
- Error messages and warnings (stderr)
- Transfer and benchmark progress (stderr)

## Implementation Phases

1. **Phase 1: Foundation** - Infrastructure, modules, context detection
2. **Phase 2: CLI Commands** - Rich output for status/worker commands
3. **Phase 3: Daemon UI** - Startup banner, health displays
4. **Phase 4: Error Experience** - Beautiful error panels
5. **Phase 5: Progress & Feedback** - Progress bars, spinners
6. **Phase 6: Polish** - Theme refinement, docs, validation

## Success Criteria

1. All rch CLI commands have beautiful, informative output
2. AI agents function identically with rich output enabled/disabled
3. NO_COLOR and FORCE_COLOR environment variables are respected
4. Performance: Zero overhead on hook classification hot path
5. All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:06.187551979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:08:31.061597056Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:08:31.061493863Z","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH. All 6 phases closed: Phase 1 (Foundation), Phase 2 (CLI Commands), Phase 3 (Daemon UI), Phase 4 (Error Experience), Phase 5 (Progress & Feedback), Phase 6 (Polish & Documentation). RCH now has beautiful, context-aware terminal output that works correctly with both humans and AI agents.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3eaa","title":"Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load + Disk)","description":"# Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load Average + Disk Space)

## Merged Scope
This bead consolidates two related worker preflight check features:
- **Load Average Guard** (formerly bd-2hbf): Skip workers with high CPU load
- **Disk Space Guard** (formerly bd-1mij): Skip workers with low free disk

Both are preflight checks that improve worker selection quality by filtering out unhealthy workers before job assignment.

## Background
Even if slots are available, a worker may be unsuitable:
- High load average spikes hurt build times
- Low disk space can cause builds to fail or slow dramatically

Preflight guards prevent wasted transfers and improve overall compilation success rates.

## Goals
1. Skip workers whose load average exceeds a threshold (per-core normalized)
2. Skip workers whose free disk space is below minimum
3. Provide clear selection reasons (NoWorkersWithCapacity, NoWorkersWithDisk)
4. Fail-open: if all workers exceed thresholds, pick best available with warning

## Design / Approach

### Configuration
```toml
[selection]
max_load_per_core = 2.0    # Skip if load1 / cores > threshold
min_free_gb = 10.0         # Skip if free disk < threshold
```

### Telemetry Fields
Extend worker capabilities/telemetry:
- `load_avg_1`, `load_avg_5`, `load_avg_15` (from /proc/loadavg or uptime)
- `disk_free_gb`, `disk_total_gb` (from df or statfs)

### Selection Logic
1. Filter by slot availability
2. Filter by load-per-core threshold
3. Filter by disk space threshold
4. Sort by SpeedScore
5. If all filtered out, pick best available with warning

## Tasks / Subtasks
1. Extend telemetry payload to include load avg and disk metrics
2. Add fallback SSH probe (uptime, df) when telemetry unavailable
3. Add config fields with validation
4. Update selection logic with reason codes
5. Expose in `rch status` and `rch workers list` output
6. Add warnings when using degraded workers

## Tests

### Unit Tests
- `rchd/src/selection.rs`: load-per-core threshold logic, disk space filter
- `rchd/src/telemetry.rs`: load avg parsing, disk metric extraction
- Selection reason codes for each guard type

### Integration Tests
- Mock worker reports high load -> skipped
- Mock worker reports low disk -> skipped
- All workers degraded -> pick best with warning

### E2E Tests
Script: `scripts/e2e_preflight_guards.sh`

Scenarios:
- Mixed load -> skip high, select low
- Mixed disk -> skip low-disk, select adequate
- All high load -> select least-loaded with warning
- All low disk -> select most-available with warning
- Status output displays load and disk warnings

## Acceptance Criteria
- High-load workers are skipped automatically
- Low-disk workers are skipped automatically
- Selection reasons are visible and actionable
- Thresholds are configurable and documented
- Fail-open behavior preserves system usability

## Logging & E2E
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, duration_ms, result, error
- Phases: classify, select (with guard reasons), sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary

## Edge Cases & UX
- Missing telemetry -> allow worker but warn
- Use load-per-core to normalize across heterogeneous machines
- Support per-worker threshold overrides
- Selection reason includes specific guard signals

## Dependencies
- Depends on bd-155i (Worker capabilities report + mismatch warnings)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"WhiteLake","created_at":"2026-01-26T00:30:20.175037620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:28.801894925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:28.801827188Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eaa","depends_on_id":"bd-155i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"bd-3eaa","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Progress - Phase 1 Complete

### Changes Made

1. **WorkerCapabilities extended** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - Added health metrics: `num_cpus`, `load_avg_1`, `load_avg_5`, `load_avg_15`
   - Added disk metrics: `disk_free_gb`, `disk_total_gb`
   - Added helper methods: `load_per_core()`, `is_high_load()`, `is_low_disk()`

2. **SelectionConfig extended** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - Added `max_load_per_core: Option<f64>` (default: 2.0)
   - Added `min_free_gb: Option<f64>` (default: 10.0)

3. **Worker probing** (rch-wkr/src/main.rs)
   - Added `probe_num_cpus()` - nproc (Linux) or sysctl (macOS)
   - Added `probe_load_average()` - /proc/loadavg or uptime
   - Added `probe_disk_space()` - df -P -k /tmp

4. **Selection logic** (rchd/src/selection.rs)
   - Added preflight filtering in `get_eligible_workers()`
   - Workers exceeding thresholds are skipped with debug logging
   - Workers kept for fail-open fallback

5. **Unit tests** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - test_load_per_core_calculation
   - test_is_high_load
   - test_is_low_disk
   - test_selection_config_preflight_defaults
   - test_selection_config_preflight_serde
   - test_worker_capabilities_health_metrics_serde

### All Tests Passing
- rch-common: 723 tests
- rchd: 23 tests
- All crates compile

### Remaining
- CLI output: Show load/disk info in `rch workers list` (optional)
- E2E test script","created_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3emi","title":"Unit Tests: Common Library (No Mocks)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for rch-common library without mock infrastructure.

-{"id":"bd-3ewx","title":"Split commands.rs into modular command files","description":"# Split commands.rs into Modules

## Problem
rch/src/commands.rs is 10,432 lines - too large to navigate, review, or maintain effectively.

## Current Structure
```
commands.rs
├── Response types (lines 93-582, ~490 lines)
├── Helper functions (lines 150-250, 396-416, ~120 lines)
└── Command implementations (lines 600-10432, ~9800 lines)
    ├── Config commands (~800 lines)
    ├── Daemon commands (~600 lines)
    ├── Workers commands (~2000 lines)
    ├── Fleet commands (~1000 lines)
    ├── Hook commands (~500 lines)
    ├── Queue/Cancel commands (~400 lines)
    ├── Self-test commands (~800 lines)
    ├── Update commands (~500 lines)
    ├── Agents commands (~300 lines)
    └── Misc helpers (~2900 lines)
```

## Proposed Structure
```
rch/src/commands/
├── mod.rs              # Re-exports, shared imports
├── types.rs            # Response structs (WorkerInfo, etc.)
├── helpers.rs          # Shared utilities
├── daemon.rs           # start, stop, restart, status, logs
├── workers.rs          # list, probe, drain, enable, benchmark, etc.
├── config.rs           # show, init, validate, set, lint, etc.
├── fleet.rs            # deploy, drain, status, upgrade
├── hook.rs             # install, uninstall, test
├── queue.rs            # queue, cancel
├── self_test.rs        # self-test command
├── update.rs           # update command
└── agents.rs           # agents commands
```

## Migration Strategy

### Phase 1: Extract Types (no behavior change)
1. Create commands/types.rs
2. Move all response structs
3. Update imports in commands.rs
4. Verify tests pass

### Phase 2: Extract Helpers
1. Create commands/helpers.rs
2. Move shared functions
3. Make public what's needed
4. Verify tests pass

### Phase 3: Extract Command Groups (one at a time)
1. Create commands/daemon.rs
2. Move daemon command handlers
3. Re-export from mod.rs
4. Verify tests pass
5. Repeat for each command group

## Public API Preservation
The extraction must not change the public API:
- Same function signatures
- Same error types
- Same command behavior
- Same test coverage

## Files to Create
- rch/src/commands/mod.rs
- rch/src/commands/types.rs
- rch/src/commands/helpers.rs
- rch/src/commands/daemon.rs
- rch/src/commands/workers.rs
- rch/src/commands/config.rs
- rch/src/commands/fleet.rs
- rch/src/commands/hook.rs
- rch/src/commands/queue.rs
- rch/src/commands/self_test.rs
- rch/src/commands/update.rs
- rch/src/commands/agents.rs

## Files to Delete (after migration)
- rch/src/commands.rs (replaced by commands/mod.rs)

## Testing
- All existing tests must pass
- No new warnings from clippy
- Build time should not increase significantly","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:11.313521582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:47:39.069980549Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","depends_on_id":"bd-lym6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Each extracted module needs its own test file:
1. `rch/src/commands/types_test.rs` - All type serde roundtrips
2. `rch/src/commands/helpers_test.rs` - All helper functions
3. `rch/src/commands/daemon_test.rs` - Daemon command handlers
4. `rch/src/commands/workers_test.rs` - Worker command handlers
5. `rch/src/commands/config_test.rs` - Config command handlers

### Integration Tests
1. All existing tests must continue passing
2. No new clippy warnings
3. Module imports work correctly

### Regression Test Script (scripts/test_commands_refactor.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_refactor_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Testing commands.rs refactor"

# Test 1: All tests pass
log "Test 1: cargo test"
cargo test -p rch 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 2: No clippy warnings
log "Test 2: cargo clippy"
cargo clippy -p rch --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 3: No unused imports/dead code
log "Test 3: Check for dead code"
cargo check -p rch 2>&1 | grep -i "unused\|dead_code" && {
  log "WARN: Unused code detected"
}

# Test 4: API unchanged
log "Test 4: Verify public API unchanged"
cargo doc -p rch --no-deps 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 5: Module file sizes
log "Test 5: Verify no file exceeds 2000 lines"
for f in rch/src/commands/*.rs; do
  lines=$(wc -l < "$f")
  if [ "$lines" -gt 2000 ]; then
    log "FAIL: $f has $lines lines (max 2000)"
    exit 1
  fi
  log "OK: $f has $lines lines"
done

log "Refactor tests complete"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Document module structure in code comments
- Log module load times at TRACE level (for build analysis)","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:58:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3fda","title":"Standardize TUI status indicators using StatusIndicator enum","description":"# Standardize TUI Status Indicators

## Problem
The TUI uses multiple icon sources inconsistently:

### In widgets.rs lines 154-160 (header status):
```rust
let status_text = match state.daemon.status {
    Status::Running => "● Running",
    Status::Stopped => "○ Stopped",
    Status::Error => "✗ Error",
    Status::Unknown => "? Unknown",
};
```

### In widgets.rs lines 237-242 (worker status):
```rust
let status_icon = match w.status {
    WorkerStatus::Healthy => "●",
    WorkerStatus::Degraded => "◐",
    WorkerStatus::Unreachable => "○",
    WorkerStatus::Draining => "◑",
};
```

### In CLI commands (using StatusIndicator):
```rust
StatusIndicator::Success.display(style)  // Returns themed ✓
StatusIndicator::Error.display(style)    // Returns themed ✗
```

## Impact
- Inconsistent appearance between TUI and CLI
- TUI doesn't respect theme settings
- Hardcoded symbols don't adapt to terminal capabilities
- Accessibility features in StatusIndicator not used

## Solution
1. Import StatusIndicator into TUI widgets
2. Create TUI-specific display method that returns &str for ratatui
3. Replace all hardcoded symbols with StatusIndicator calls
4. Ensure colors are sourced from ColorScheme

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs
- rch/src/ui/theme.rs (may need TUI-specific display method)

## Testing
- Verify TUI matches CLI appearance
- Test with --high-contrast mode
- Test with color-blind palettes
- Verify symbol consistency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:16.913248958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:33:33.504121012Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:33:33.504057734Z","close_reason":"Standardized TUI status indicators using StatusIndicator enum and Symbols struct. Replaced all hardcoded symbols in widgets.rs (daemon header, worker status, active builds, build history) with centralized Symbols::UNICODE and StatusIndicator-based lookups. Added indicator_color helper for ratatui Color bridging. All symbols now respect theme/accessibility settings. 5 new integration tests. All 1719 tests pass, clippy clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fda","depends_on_id":"bd-lcoa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3fda","depends_on_id":"bd-ryot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
